---
title: "Experiments Dashboard"
---

## What — Experiment Suite

- **Experiment suite**: three experiments systematically validate each claim in `claims.qmd`
- **Main Experiment**: full model vs. all baselines on held-out test set
- **Ablation Study**: component-by-component contribution analysis
- **HP Sensitivity**: effect of key hyperparameters on performance and robustness

## Why — Validation Strategy

- **Why three experiments**: each targets a different claim type — superiority (main), necessity (ablation), robustness (HP)
- **Why this ordering**: main experiment confirms the headline result; ablation explains it; HP search stress-tests it
- **Reproducibility**: `seed=42` fixed in all runs; results reported as mean ± std over [K] seeds
- **Compute budget**: [e.g., ~N GPU-hours on single A100 / RTX 4090]

```{dot}
//| label: fig-exp-design
//| fig-cap: "Validation strategy: claims → experiments → metrics → confirmed claims"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 3.5
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=TB
  graph [ranksep=0.55, nodesep=0.5]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.6, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph row1 {
    rank=same
    C1 [label="Claim 1", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    C2 [label="Claim 2", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    C3 [label="Claim 3", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph row2 {
    rank=same
    Main [label="Main Expt"]
    Abl  [label="Ablation"]
    HP   [label="HP Search"]
  }
  subgraph row3 {
    rank=same
    M1 [label="Primary Metric"]
    M2 [label="Delta per Comp"]
    M3 [label="Sensitivity"]
  }
  subgraph row4 {
    rank=same
    Out [label="Validated Claims", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c", width=5.0]
  }

  C1 -> Main  C2 -> Abl  C3 -> HP
  Main -> M1  Abl -> M2  HP -> M3
  M1 -> Out  M2 -> Out  M3 -> Out
}
```

<details>
<summary><strong>How — Datasets</strong></summary>

- **Name**: [dataset name and version]
- **Size**: [N_train / N_val / N_test] samples; [D] features; [C] classes / continuous target
- **Source**: [origin — UCI / Kaggle / custom / synthetic]
- **Preprocessing**: standardisation, missing-value handling, class-imbalance strategy
- **Splits**: [stratified k-fold / random / time-based] — consistent across all experiments

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>How — Baselines</strong></summary>

| Baseline | Description | Reference |
|----------|-------------|-----------|
| Linear | Logistic regression | scikit-learn |
| MLP-vanilla | MLP without key components | this repo |
| [SotA method] | [description] | [citation] |

- All baselines trained with identical optimiser, lr, and seed
- Hyperparameters for each baseline tuned with the same Optuna budget

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>How — Evaluation Metrics</strong></summary>

| Metric | Formula / Tool | Used In |
|--------|---------------|---------|
| [Metric A] | [e.g., accuracy, top-1] | Main, Ablation |
| [Metric B] | [e.g., macro F1] | Main, Ablation |
| [Metric C] | [e.g., ECE — calibration] | Main |
| Δ vs. Full | metric drop when component removed | Ablation |
| Sensitivity σ | std across HP range | HP Search |

- Statistical test: [test name] for significance, p < 0.05
- Effect size: Cohen's d or absolute Δ reported alongside p-value

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>How — MLflow Tracking</strong></summary>

All runs automatically logged to `mlruns/`. To view:

```bash
mlflow ui --backend-store-uri mlruns/
# Open http://localhost:5000
```

Key logged artifacts: `config.yaml`, confusion matrix PNG, per-class metrics CSV, checkpoint path.

</details>

---

## Experiment Suite

::: {.card-grid}
<div class="nav-card">
<a href="main.qmd">Main Experiment</a>
<p>Full model vs. all baselines on held-out test set</p>
</div>
<div class="nav-card">
<a href="ablation.qmd">Ablation Study</a>
<p>Component-by-component contribution analysis</p>
</div>
<div class="nav-card">
<a href="hyperparam.qmd">HP Sensitivity</a>
<p>Effect of key hyperparameters on performance</p>
</div>
:::
